title: soap_post_request
text: soap_post_request Receives the SOAP envelope containing the reply that Uniface produced and returns the SOAP envelope to be sent as a reply to the Web server. #include <wsciapi.h> int soap_post_request(char *vars, unsigned char *in, unsigned char **out, int *len, void **context) Parameters *vars —unnamed Uniface list that contains a named list (SERVERVARIABLES) of server variables supported by the Uniface Web Request Dispatcher (WRD). This named list contains information gathered in the web server about the request. It is a null-terminated string in the UTF-8 character set. For more information, see Server Variables for Pre- and Post-Requests . *in —pointer to the web service response SOAP envelope as produced by Uniface. This envelope is in UTF-8 character set, it is null-terminated, and its length is available in the len parameter. **out —address of a pointer that will receive one of the following: Pointer to an amended SOAP envelope—if the SOAP envelope is amended, its length should be set in the len parameter. If an amended SOAP envelope is returned, it is assumed that the buffer is dynamically allocated, and, if the implementation provides a soap_rel_buf() function, it will be called when the envelope is no longer needed. For soap_pre_request , the amended SOAP envelope should be in UTF-8 or UTF-16 character sets. Uniface will continue processing as if it had received the amended SOAP envelope from the Web server. For soap_post_request , the amended SOAP envelope should be in the UTF-8 character set. It will be returned to the client application as if Uniface had produced the response. Error message—for an error return status ( 1 ), a null-terminated error message that will be packaged into a SOAP Fault response and returned to the client application. In this case, the buffer is assumed not to be dynamically allocated and soap_rel_buf() is not called. This allows allocation errors to be reported. An implementation does not need to amend the SOAP envelope, in which case the pointer should be set to NULL. *len —pointer to an integer. Initially the integer contains the length of the in parameter, and an implementation should set the integer to the length of the amended SOAP envelope in the out parameter. **context —The implementation may allocate memory for itself to use and should place the address of that memory in this pointer. The pointer will be delivered to the implementation on every call. In this way, the implementation can store data from one call to the next. When Uniface is exiting, a call to soap_rel_buf() will be made to free this memory. Return Values The function receives a Uniface list consisting of web server variables and the web service request in the form of a SOAP envelope. 0 —success. The function can return an amended SOAP envelope that Uniface can proceed to use. If a new envelope is not returned, then the original one is used. 1 —error. The implementation can return a null-terminated error message. This error message will be packed into a SOAP Fault by Uniface and returned to the caller. Related Links SOAP Faults SOAP messages are XML-based, and the SOAP protocol defines a SOAP Fault element for returning remote errors to the caller. Was this topic helpful? Like Dislike
